# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    @products = Product.all
    search = params.fetch(:search, {}).to_h

    where_clause = []
    where_args = {}

    if search[:name].present?
      where_clause << "products.name ILIKE :name"
      where_args[:name] = "%#{search[:name]}%"
    end

    if search[:category].present?
      where_clause << "products.category = :category"
      where_args[:category] = search[:category]
    end

    if search[:min_price].present?
      where_clause << "products.price_cents >= :min_price"
      where_args[:min_price] = (BigDecimal(search[:min_price].to_s) * 100).to_i
    end

    if search[:max_price].present?
      where_clause << "products.price_cents <= :max_price"
      where_args[:max_price] = (BigDecimal(search[:max_price].to_s) * 100).to_i
    end

    if where_clause.any?
      @products = @products.where(where_clause.join(" AND "), where_args)
    end

    render :search
  end
end
